voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: I thought maybe about submitting this song as well as Beach Boys California girls just thought it would be interesting to hear the back to back. But felt there were stronger Beach Boys options to submit from Surfin USA, Good Vibrations, Kokomo. Or even next to Carolina Girls.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: This one sounded really familiar to me, so I had to look up if it was based on another song that I might know. Turns out, no, it's an original... but it did lead me to discover that it was created as a West Coast answer to Jay-Z and Alicia Keys' NYC anthem "Empire State of Mind" (which is a pretty good song in its own right).A little too poppy for my taste, but I did enjoy reading up on it since it's a pretty odd collab.

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: Also tried to submit this one, it's classic summer/beachy vibes

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Trying so hard to get the beach from this, but I just kept getting a car ride. Just felt like cruisin' 'round town. Then, suddenly, beach volleyball out of nowhere... That's worth a point for sure.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Lol, Snoop what're you doing in there

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I think I forgot Snoop Dogg was in this!? Takes me straight back to high school

27.title: It's About Family
27.description: Songs with a family member in the title (i.e. mother, brother, father, cousin, grandmother)

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: This feels so unique in this playlist. This is definitely far from his most popular song but I can't say that I know any of the other songs. There are parts that feel familiar but I think that it might just be because it's the popular style at the moment. I can think of songs it kind of sounds like but can't pin it to one song or artist. Closest I get is See You Again by Wiz Khalifa.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Absolutely loved this. Hi Lyndsay!!

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I don't think I've ever heard this song because I usually don't listen to country, but I love the lyrics! "I think y'all means all" is so cute 😌

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: So, I found this song on TikTok a while ago and it was used a lot by people who, like me, grew up country, but were generally progressive and it just kind of made me feel seen? Like, it's a really weird dicotomy to grow up with a lot of these experiences and such that aren't bad, but are almost always lumped in with crazy boomers from the same area. Being country or in my case, a domesticated county person, doesn't mean you are a terrible person or that everything you grew up with is trash.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: An interesting unexpected entry. Definitely didn't think I'd be hearing any country in this playlist.

voters.comment: I went and listened to the other version and did indeed like it much better. I actually don't care that much for the original...

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I appreciated the inclusion of more heavy stuff this week! I've seen Beartooth on local music festival lineups before but never took the time to listen to them. I really liked this song

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Oh I like this! And always like supporting an Ohio artist

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: This band sounded so familiar to me so I just had to dig up some info on them. Eventually, I found that the guy's previous band toured with bands like Showbread, which is probably where I think I'm recognizing the sound. The guy who started the original band had like three other bands that all kicked him out. I guess you can figure out who the problem is in that situation.

all_songs.name: Ring of Fire
all_songs.album: Ring Of Fire: The Best Of Johnny Cash

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Definitely interesting to consider how different this is from modern country, I prefer this style way more lol

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: The breakup week is gone now. Didn't like the energy it assumed. Also, DIXIE CHICKS JUMPSCARE! I thought the song was okay but when they said he had to die this shit went to 11.

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: Does murder count as a breakup? Also the backlash they got in 2003 was some absolute bullshit.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: VERY big Fried Green Tomatoes vibes, absolutely loved it

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Is this Lindsay or did someone else submit a country song to trick us🧐 I love it

voters.name: Ola
voters.comment: get his ass!!!

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Ohhh man, going back and listening to P!nk now - so many good songs, two of my favorites when they came out were "Sober" and "Just like a Pill." Fun to hear that backstory about the Try This album, this song definitely sounds kinda different from her other stuff but I still like it

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I swore this was a cover. Amanda argued it wasn't, and she was right. It was originally written by the singer of Rancid, but when it didn't fit their sound, he shared it with P!nk and changed it up to fit her sound.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Not my fav, but still a goodie.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Gotta give another point to the other BDylan submitter 👌 Even if this is not one of my favs.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: This is probably my favorite Bob Dylan song so I'm happy you submitted it even though someone else already submitted one from him! The times are always a-changin' and this will always be a great political anthem

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I really don't like Bob Dylan's voice, but I love this song! It was used super well in the movie Watchmen and it always reminds me of that any time I hear it. There's actually a Floggy Molly cover that I much prefer, but gotta give ol Bob respect for the original

voters.name: Ola
voters.comment: ALSO makes me think of watchmen (2009) lol

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: Need cringe I guess depending on the audience, but you have a friend here. I enjoy these types of things.efwst

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: As a fellow lover of cringe video game songs, I commend you for showing up to the assignment while also picking something that was genuinely catchy!! Well done, you.

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: Listen this was genuinely catchy and is stuck in my head so it gets a vote for that reason alone.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: As a long time Nate Wants to Battle fan, I am very familiar with video game fan songs. I have such a visceral reaction to anything FNAF now. The internet has very much ruined any level of enjoyment I could have derived from this franchise.

voters.comment: Love the beat on this one 🤘🤘

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Definitely has a 100 Gecs kind of sound to it. I wanted to know more about it was fascinated to find this on Wikipedia: "Platt claimed in a Genius interview that the song, made with GarageBand, was a soundfont test for Pokémon Black and White. Platt also stated that the song covered many of his issues; how tired he was of the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, his future, and his gender dysphoria." Cool song.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Welp this song is from 2008 so I agree, it's definitely feeling nostalgic to me - though I don't remember any specific songs by Bullet for My Valentine, the band name definitely sounds familiar. It's reminding me of some of the bands I liked in High School, like All American Rejects, Cartel, Taking Back Sunday... ahhhh now I need to go listen to all of those guys again!

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I've definitely heard of them but I don't think I've ever listened to a single song of theirs before. I had heard good things about them. That guitar riff out of the intro kicks ass. This would rule as a boss fight theme or wrestling entrance theme.

voters.comment: they sound like literally any band from 2008 (ain't mad about it)

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: lol

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: Definitely fits the vibes for an outdoor party and I'm always down for Train

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: Definitely still sing to this one, an oldy.

voters.comment: I will not do the Hustle but will pass along a vote. No matter how threatening the opening is, I will not "do it."

Like Cupid Shuffle, Cha Cha Slide, and Wobble. I have always called all of those "The..." But you know one that would have worked? The Hokey Pokey.

voters.comment: I dig this song but I feel like it can't ever decide what genre it wants to be 😭 "gimme that RIDE"

voters.comment: I like slow moody girl songs so I like dis. The lyrics are very interesting. Never heard of her, I should check out her other stuff!

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: Really enjoyed the way she plays with the tempo at the end of this one, it's such an interesting move

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: Bi representation for the win!

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Love that bit at the end when it starts picking up the tempo.

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: Funny thing is that when last playlist was over this would frequently come on, so I already had heard it a bunch between them and now.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Shaaba!! Starting to RLY dig her, this song was an absolute bop

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: A nice chill ending to the playlist, it's catchy!

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: This is not the last of Shaaba you will see in this league.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: I dig this song but I feel like it can't ever decide what genre it wants to be 😭 "gimme that RIDE"

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: It's kinda funny because I feel like this week's playlist has a lot more techno in it than usual, and now this song doesn't stand out as much to me, BUT I do I appreciate the branching out in genres, as this has a country-pop vibe to it too

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: Saa and I agreed that this song can't decide what genre it wants to be. Is it pop, country, rock, electronic? It's wants to be all of them.

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: And apparently you nutters agreed, cause god dang y'all dropped some bangers this week!

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: I thought maybe about submitting this song as well as Beach Boys California girls just thought it would be interesting to hear the back to back. But felt there were stronger Beach Boys options to submit from Surfin USA, Good Vibrations, Kokomo. Or even next to Carolina Girls.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: This one sounded really familiar to me, so I had to look up if it was based on another song that I might know. Turns out, no, it's an original... but it did lead me to discover that it was created as a West Coast answer to Jay-Z and Alicia Keys' NYC anthem "Empire State of Mind" (which is a pretty good song in its own right).A little too poppy for my taste, but I did enjoy reading up on it since it's a pretty odd collab.

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: Also tried to submit this one, it's classic summer/beachy vibes

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Trying so hard to get the beach from this, but I just kept getting a car ride. Just felt like cruisin' 'round town. Then, suddenly, beach volleyball out of nowhere... That's worth a point for sure.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Lol, Snoop what're you doing in there

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I think I forgot Snoop Dogg was in this!? Takes me straight back to high school

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: I like it, but not sure about beach vibes 🤔

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I think I don't care for how it feels like the singer is yelling? But definitely can see the pirate beach vibes!

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: AND it has backing vocals during the appropriate call/response section of the chorus! Oddly, most other versions I have heard are missing the middle 2 or 3 verses. Like, yeah, interest in naval history is niche, which is probably why others leave them out, but I kind of like that about the song, lol.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: The breakup week is gone now. Didn't like the energy it assumed. Also, DIXIE CHICKS JUMPSCARE! I thought the song was okay but when they said he had to die this shit went to 11.

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: Does murder count as a breakup? Also the backlash they got in 2003 was some absolute bullshit.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: VERY big Fried Green Tomatoes vibes, absolutely loved it

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Is this Lindsay or did someone else submit a country song to trick us🧐 I love it

voters.name: Ola
voters.comment: get his ass!!!

voters.name: Rick
voters.comment: 80s hair metal is always a soft spot for me.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: This song tells a very intriguing story. I kept wanting to hear more!

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Lmao two Warrant songs in my leagues at once!!

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Ohhh man, going back and listening to P!nk now - so many good songs, two of my favorites when they came out were "Sober" and "Just like a Pill." Fun to hear that backstory about the Try This album, this song definitely sounds kinda different from her other stuff but I still like it

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I swore this was a cover. Amanda argued it wasn't, and she was right. It was originally written by the singer of Rancid, but when it didn't fit their sound, he shared it with P!nk and changed it up to fit her sound.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Not my fav, but still a goodie.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: This one takes the crown as my favorite new discovery this week. Among an exceptionally strong playlist, that's a pretty high honor!

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Great sound!

voters.name: Rick
voters.comment: Some heavier stuff! My style for sure! I love the Oni song with Howard Jones.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: It's funny this came after Bells because I was just thinking how much this reminded me of August Burns Red and bands with similar sounds.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Oh I fuck bigly with Beartooth.

voters.name: Rick
voters.comment: Beartooth is always welcomed. Saw them at Kemba a few years back, such an energetic show.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I appreciated the inclusion of more heavy stuff this week! I've seen Beartooth on local music festival lineups before but never took the time to listen to them. I really liked this song

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Oh I like this! And always like supporting an Ohio artist

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: This band sounded so familiar to me so I just had to dig up some info on them. Eventually, I found that the guy's previous band toured with bands like Showbread, which is probably where I think I'm recognizing the sound. The guy who started the original band had like three other bands that all kicked him out. I guess you can figure out who the problem is in that situation.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I will not do the Hustle but will pass along a vote. No matter how threatening the opening is, I will not "do it."

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Thank you, this did indeed make me laugh!Also thanks for making me search through a bunch of similar dance songs and being surprised that they don't start with "The." Like Cupid Shuffle, Cha Cha Slide, and Wobble. I have always called all of those "The..." But you know one that would have worked? The Hokey Pokey.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: You're two weeks early, you were supposed to save your disco for 1970s week :P

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: A true classic t b h

songs.name: Ring of Fire
songs.album: Ring Of Fire: The Best Of Johnny Cash

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Definitely interesting to consider how different this is from modern country, I prefer this style way more lol

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Gotta give another point to the other BDylan submitter 👌 Even if this is not one of my favs.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: This is probably my favorite Bob Dylan song so I'm happy you submitted it even though someone else already submitted one from him! The times are always a-changin' and this will always be a great political anthem

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I really don't like Bob Dylan's voice, but I love this song! It was used super well in the movie Watchmen and it always reminds me of that any time I hear it. There's actually a Floggy Molly cover that I much prefer, but gotta give ol Bob respect for the original

voters.name: Ola
voters.comment: ALSO makes me think of watchmen (2009) lol
